message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Extending click.edit() to support editing multiple files via filename param accepting str or iterable of str

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__/edit_file (subprocess creationflags on Windows, os.nice preexec_fn on Unix) and to termui.edit() signature/docstring, forwarded to Editor(). Only additive changes near Editor class and edit() signature/body - should merge cleanly with your filename iterable work as long as you keep the process_priority param and its pass-through when you touch edit()/Editor.__init__.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) to edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) joining multiple quoted paths for subprocess; termui.edit() filename param now accepts str|Iterable[str] with overloads. If your process_priority patch still calls edit_file, update the call site to edit_files((filename,)) to avoid clashing with the rename.
